payload.answer: Do not store the 500-row CSV table as durable memory. Large raw tabular data should not be committed row-by-row to personal memory unless the user explicitly asks to remember a specific durable fact, preference, summary, or named artifact. If storage is needed, store only a concise description or reference to the table, preserving ambiguity and avoiding fabrication of row-level facts.

entity_resolution.reason: The input is a large CSV-style table fixture with repeated id,name,value rows and no durable user preference, identity fact, relationship, or long-term semantic memory to preserve.

details: Recognized input as a large repeated CSV table fixture.
No durable personal memory was created.
Avoided committing 500 raw rows as memory cards.

repair_options: Ask the user whether they want a summary, analysis, or an external/document-style reference retained instead of storing all rows.
If a specific row or derived fact is important, request the exact fact to remember.
Store a compact artifact pointer or metadata summary only if the system supports non-memory document storage.
